question: Write code to evaluate a chess position. It can accept chess position as an input in any convenient format, and output evaluation to clearly indicate which side (white or black) has an advantage, and numerically measure this advantage. Adhere to common values of pieces (pawn - 1, knight and bishop - 3, rook - 5, queen - 9), as well as other useful metrics, e.g. number of controlled squares, number of active moves, check and mate possibilities, and so on.

The position will be inserted as an input with any preferred type, with an evaluation as output to explicitly indicate the advantageous color (white or black), accompanied by a numerical expression to highlight the advantage. It's also good to rely on well-known values of pieces (pawn - 1, knight and bishop - 3, rook - 5, queen - 9), alongside important measures, for instance, the sum of controlled tiles, sum of current plays, potential to check and checkmate, and related.
paraphraser.0.9: Formulate an algorithm to judge a position in chess. The input can be any form of capturing position of chess piece and location. The output will numerically rate each sides' overall position; whether black or white is ahead on the board. Typical material count will be used: pawn equates to one, knight and bishop are equivalent with three, a rook has value of five and a queen scores at nine. A side's advantage is more than their pieces, e.g. area controlled, total number of possible chess moves, options to place an opponent's king in check.
